 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.3","title":"Allowlist CLI: add/list/remove/validate (project + user)","description":"## Objective

Implement `dcg allowlist` CLI commands so users can fix false positives without editing TOML by hand.

## Commands (v1)

Primary namespace:

- `dcg allowlist add <rule_id> --reason "..." [--project|--user] [--expires ...] [--condition KEY=VAL]`
- `dcg allowlist add-command "<cmd>" --reason "..." [--project|--user] [--expires ...]`
- `dcg allowlist list [--project|--user]`
- `dcg allowlist remove <rule_id|entry_id> [--project|--user]`
- `dcg allowlist validate [--project|--user]`

Ergonomic aliases (high-value):

- `dcg allow <rule_id> --reason "..." [--project|--user]` → alias of `allowlist add`
- `dcg unallow <rule_id|entry_id> [--project|--user]` → alias of `allowlist remove`

Rationale: deny messages can print shorter copy/paste commands.

## Behavioral requirements

- Default scope should be explicit:
  - either require `--project|--user`, or default to project if in a git repo and file exists
- All mutations must preserve TOML readability (stable ordering; keep comments where feasible).
- CLI should set audit metadata automatically:
  - `added_by` (from env/user)
  - `added_at` (RFC3339)

## Acceptance criteria

- CLI can create `.dcg/allowlist.toml` if missing.
- CLI mutations are idempotent and stable (no reordering chaos).
- Validate prints actionable errors/warnings.

## Tests

- CLI integration tests for add/list/remove (including alias commands).
- Validation tests for:
  - expired entries
  - missing reason
  - unknown rule id

All quality gates pass (463 tests, clippy, fmt).","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:04:59.51609388-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:21:56.560137459-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.4","title":"Allowlist: conditions, expiration, and wildcard safety rules","description":"## Objective

Implement the more advanced allowlist semantics in a way that is safe and predictable.

## Semantics to support

### 1) Expiration

- `expires_at` field on entries.
- Expired entries:
  - evaluator: ignored
  - validate: warning (or error if `--strict`)

### 2) Conditions (environment gating)

- `conditions = { KEY = "VAL" }` maps to environment variables.
- Conditions are ANDed.
- Missing env var means condition not met.

### 3) Wildcards

- Allow `pack_id:*` (scoped to one pack).
- Disallow `*:*`.
- Allow `pack_id:prefix-*` only if we commit to stable naming conventions.

### 4) Risk acknowledgement

- Regex allowlist entries require `risk_acknowledged=true`.
- Validation should warn on overly broad regex (heuristics).

## Acceptance criteria

- Expiration works and is test-covered.
- Conditions work (CI-only allowlists etc).
- Wildcard constraints prevent unsafe global bypass.

## Tests

- Expiration tests using fixed timestamps.
- Condition tests with env var manipulation.
- Wildcard constraint tests.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.5","title":"Allowlist: comprehensive tests + documentation","description":"## Objective

Make allowlisting safe in practice by shipping:

- comprehensive automated tests
- clear user documentation

## Tests

### Parser/validation

- invalid TOML does not crash hook
- missing required fields surfaced in validate
- risky entries require acknowledgement

### Evaluator integration

- allowlist overrides only the intended rule
- allowlisting does not disable other packs
- allowlist layer precedence works

### CLI

- add/list/remove idempotence
- validate output contains actionable messages

## Docs

- README section: "Resolving false positives" describing:
  - `dcg explain`
  - `dcg allowlist add ...`
  - recommended allowlist shapes
  - security caveats

## Acceptance criteria

- Tests cover the core allowlist behaviors and guard against regressions.
- Docs are sufficient that a user can resolve a false positive without reading source.

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.6","title":"Allowlist: E2E coverage in scripts/e2e_test.sh (hook path)","description":"## Objective

Add end-to-end coverage proving allowlists affect the **real hook path** (stdin JSON → decision) and do not create accidental broad bypasses.

## Why E2E is required

Allowlisting is a high-risk feature:

- It must override only the intended rule.
- It must work in hook mode (not just `dcg test`).
- It must not silently disable packs or normalization.

## Test cases (must include)

1) Baseline: a known catastrophic command is blocked
- e.g., `git reset --hard` (record the rule_id used)

2) Project allowlist overrides that exact rule
- Create `.dcg/allowlist.toml` in an isolated fixture repo/cwd.
- Re-run the same hook JSON input.
- Expected: ALLOW (or WARN if configured) with **no JSON deny**.

3) Non-target rule remains enforced
- Ensure another blocked command is still blocked (e.g., `git clean -fd`).

4) Expired allowlist entry does not apply

5) Unsafe wildcard entry is rejected (or requires risk_ack)

## Logging requirements

E2E output should print:

- the rule_id being allowlisted
- which allowlist layer was used (project vs user)
- expected vs actual decision

## Acceptance criteria

- E2E fails if allowlist does not apply in hook mode.
- E2E fails if allowlist is broader than intended.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.2.2` evaluator integration
- `git_safety_guard-1gt.2.3` allowlist CLI (optional; direct file edit acceptable in test)

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3","title":"Add decision modes: block vs warn vs log-only","description":"## Objective

Reduce disruption by allowing less-confident detections to warn or log instead of hard-blocking, while keeping high-confidence matches as hard blocks.

## Background

Some detections are extremely high confidence (e.g., `rm -rf /`, `git reset --hard`). Others are context-dependent or susceptible to false positives.

A single binary mode (always deny) can be overly disruptive, especially when expanding into heredoc scanning and AST-based heuristics.

## Proposed behavior

Add a policy layer that can choose between:

- **deny** (current behavior): block command
- **warn**: print warning to stderr and/or log, but allow command (no JSON deny)
- **log-only**: silent allow, but log event for later review

Policy should be configurable:

- global default (e.g., deny)
- per-pack override
- per-pattern override

## Safety constraints

- Default should remain deny for known-catastrophic patterns.
- Warn/log-only must never apply to the most dangerous patterns unless the user explicitly configures it.

## Acceptance criteria

- Config supports selecting mode per pack/pattern.
- Output is correct per mode:
  - deny: stderr warning + stdout JSON deny
  - warn: stderr warning, no stdout
  - log-only: optional log entry, no stderr/stdout
- Tests cover all three modes.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:05:08.791980925-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T14:19:44.235316204-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T14:19:44.235316204-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented policy config (default/pack/rule) and hook behavior for deny/warn/log; added E2E policy coverage; Critical rules cannot be globally downgraded.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:05:08.793291122-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:06:02.265838837-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.1","title":"Decision policy: define rule severity taxonomy + default modes","description":"## Objective

Define an explicit, documented severity taxonomy for rules, and map severity → default decision mode.

This prevents ad-hoc "some things warn, some deny" drift and supports:

- predictable rollout (warn-first where appropriate)
- stable CI policy (`--fail-on error|warning` for scan)
- consistent UX between hook, explain, and scan

## Proposed taxonomy (v1)

- `error` (catastrophic / irreversible): default **DENY**
  - examples: `rm -rf /`, `git reset --hard`, `git clean -fd`, `DROP DATABASE prod`

- `warning` (dangerous but sometimes intentional): default **WARN** (or DENY in strict packs)
  - examples: `git push --force` (could be warn if confidence low)

- `info` (advisory): default **LOG-ONLY**

## Rules of the road

- Severity is a property of a rule (pack_id + pattern_name), not of user configuration.
- User configuration can *tighten* (warn→deny) freely.
- User configuration can *loosen* (deny→warn/log) only with explicit acknowledgement (see allowlist safety rules).

## Acceptance criteria

- `Severity` enum exists and is used consistently across:
  - hook deny/warn/log output
  - `dcg explain`
  - `dcg scan` JSON output
- Default mapping is documented and test-covered.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.3` (decision modes implementation)
- `git_safety_guard-99e.2` (stable rule IDs)
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:01:58.171989907-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T01:50:42.026890924-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T01:50:42.026890924-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Severity and DecisionMode enums with Critical/High/Medium/Low levels. Updated DestructivePattern, DestructiveMatch, CheckResult structs. Added 10 comprehensive tests. All 92 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:02:07.49512402-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:02:19.24160358-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.2","title":"Decision policy: tag existing rules with severity + regression tests","description":"## Objective

Apply the severity taxonomy from `git_safety_guard-1gt.3.1` to existing packs and ensure outputs stay stable.

## Scope

- Core packs (`core.git`, `core.filesystem`) first.
- High-signal packs next (docker/k8s/db) as they become reachable in hook mode.

## Requirements

- Each destructive rule has an explicit severity.
- Scan output includes severity for each finding.
- Deny/warn/log mode defaults respect severity mapping.

## Tests

- Snapshot tests for:
  - deny output includes severity
  - explain JSON includes severity
  - scan JSON includes severity
- Regression tests for key rules:
  - `core.git:reset-hard` remains `error`
  - `core.filesystem:rm-rf-non-temp` remains `error`

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.3.1` (taxonomy)
- `git_safety_guard-99e.2.2` (pattern metadata propagation)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Every rule in core packs has an explicit severity tag.
- Hook output includes severity (or decision mode derived from severity) in a stable, test-covered way.
- `dcg explain` shows severity per match and per final decision.
- `dcg scan --json` emits severity per finding and honors `--fail-on error|warning`.
- Regression tests prevent accidental severity drift for a curated set of high-impact rules.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:02:32.98122712-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T13:48:33.789940747-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T13:48:33.789940747-05:00","close_reason":"Completed severity tagging for core packs and explain output","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:02:42.227156139-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:02:54.842192671-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:02:59.882469179-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
